analysis.summary: dotnet-dev is a skill-only plugin (no commands, no hooks, no agents) containing one SKILL.md (15.6 KB) plus four reference files (~54 KB total) and a bundled Microsoft Learn MCP server declared inline in plugin.json. Versions are consistent at 1.2.0 between plugin.json and marketplace.json (plugin README carries no version/changelog at all). Content quality of the .NET/EF Core/Aspire material is high and dual-database (PostgreSQL + SQL Server) coverage is genuinely useful. The two biggest defects: (1) the skill frontmatter description was stripped of its "Use when"/trigger keywords in commit 8948bc1 (2025-12-26), weakening model-based auto-triggering, while the README still advertises those triggers; (2) MCP usage documentation in SKILL.md and references/microsoft-learn-mcp.md instructs using a nonexistent tool name (mcp__microsoft-learn__search) and an unreliable mcporter CLI fallback instead of the server's actual tools (microsoft_docs_search, microsoft_code_sample_search, microsoft_docs_fetch). Integration is entirely inbound: long-running and system-design-doc both route .NET work to /dotnet-dev, but dotnet-dev itself references no other plugin and no shared artifact (feature_list.json, design_doc_list.json, etc.).
strengths: Clean skill-only architecture with zero hooks - no SessionStart cost beyond the MCP server, nothing heavy loaded into every session

Progressive disclosure is structurally correct: 4 on-demand reference files (ef-core-patterns.md 17 KB, testing-patterns.md 17 KB, aspire-setup.md 13 KB, microsoft-learn-mcp.md 7 KB) explicitly indexed from SKILL.md lines 457-463

Version sync is correct: plugin.json (D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/dotnet-dev/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:3) and marketplace.json (D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json:43) both say 1.2.0; marketplace entry has rich category/tags metadata

Dual-database guidance (PostgreSQL vs SQL Server selection criteria, provider-specific features like JSONB/temporal tables/HierarchyId, database-agnostic configuration pattern) is concrete and practical
Bundled Microsoft Learn MCP server (plugin.json:8-13) loads correctly - its three tools are visible and usable in sessions
Reference files contain production-grade, complete code samples (Testcontainers + Respawn integration tests, Aspire AppHost orchestration, domain events, interceptors) rather than generic advice
Plugin is well-positioned in the marketplace workflow: long-running /init, /init-existing, and /continue all auto-detect .csproj/.sln and mandate the dotnet-dev skill

issues.title: Skill frontmatter lost its trigger phrases (regression in commit 8948bc1)
issues.detail: SKILL.md frontmatter description was reduced from a trigger-rich block (Use when: creating .NET projects... Triggers: ".NET", "C#", "Entity Framework", "EF Core", "migration", ...) to a single short line with no 'Use when' clause and no trigger keywords. Model-invoked skill selection depends heavily on the description; sibling plugins (qa-ui-test, flow-discovery) include explicit EN+TH trigger lists. The plugin README still promises automatic activation on those keywords (README.md:81-90), so the docs now over-promise what the frontmatter delivers. There are also no Thai triggers despite the user/marketplace being Thai-first.
issues.evidence: D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/dotnet-dev/skills/dotnet-dev/SKILL.md:3 (current short description); git show 8948bc1 diff removing the 'Use when:'/'Triggers:' block; D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/dotnet-dev/README.md:81-90 (stale trigger-keyword promise)

issues.title: MCP usage docs reference nonexistent tool names and a dubious mcporter CLI invocation
issues.detail: SKILL.md tells the model to call 'npx mcporter call --stdio "streamable-http https://learn.microsoft.com/api/mcp" search query:...' and names the tool 'mcp__microsoft-learn__search'. The bundled server actually exposes microsoft_docs_search, microsoft_code_sample_search, and microsoft_docs_fetch (prefixed mcp__plugin_dotnet-dev_microsoft-learn__* when installed). A model following these instructions will either run a failing/hanging npx command or look for a tool that does not exist, instead of using the already-loaded MCP tools. references/microsoft-learn-mcp.md repeats the same wrong tool name and dedicates a whole 'Alternative: mcporter CLI' section to it.

issues.title: README installation instructions are stale and use a wrong plugin name/command
issues.detail: Plugin README references 'your-username/dotnet-dev-skill' placeholder, a '/plugin add /path/to/dotnet-dev-skill' command that does not exist (correct: /plugin install dotnet-dev@agent-marketplace, which the repo root README gets right at README.md:65), and a 'dotnet-dev-skill' directory name that does not match plugins/dotnet-dev. The standalone-skill copy command also uses the wrong source path.

issues.title: No version or changelog anywhere in the plugin README
issues.detail: Version 1.2.0 exists only in plugin.json and marketplace.json. The README has no version badge and no changelog, so what changed in 1.1.0 (SQL Server support, per commit a257184) and 1.2.0 (path/metadata fixes, per f6ef22c/c5c35a6) is undocumented. Every other actively maintained plugin in this marketplace (ui-mockup, long-running, qa-ui-test) keeps a README changelog; this is the drift pattern already tracked in the marketplace-version-sync memory.

issues.title: SKILL.md is a 15.6 KB monolith with internal duplication against references/
issues.detail: Eight full code templates live inline in SKILL.md (lines 121-400: BaseEntity, IRepository, IUnitOfWork, generic Repository, DbContext, DI, appsettings, Aspire AppHost), making the always-loaded-on-trigger file ~480 lines. Several blocks duplicate reference content: EnableRetryOnFailure config appears in SKILL.md:300-325 and again in ef-core-patterns.md:399-410 and :559-571; audit-field stamping is implemented twice with no 'pick one' guidance (SaveChangesAsync override at SKILL.md:254-271 vs AuditableEntityInterceptor at ef-core-patterns.md:273-321) - applying both would double-stamp; Aspire AppHost appears in SKILL.md:366-400 and aspire-setup.md:34-81.

issues.title: Code-template defects: redundant soft-delete filtering and 'new' Id property hiding
issues.detail: The DbContext template installs a global query filter for IsDeleted (SKILL.md:243-251), yet every Repository method also manually filters !e.IsDeleted (GetByIdAsync:184-185, GetAllAsync:187-188, ExistsAsync:209-210, Query:212-213) - redundant and confusing about which mechanism is authoritative. BaseEntity<TKey> uses 'public new TKey Id' (SKILL.md:135-138), shadowing the base long Id; property hiding via 'new' breaks EF Core mapping/polymorphism expectations and is a known anti-pattern that a generated project would inherit.

issues.title: Three contradictory MCP configuration methods across plugin.json, README, and reference doc
issues.detail: plugin.json uses 'npx -y mcp-remote <url>' (stdio bridge spawning a node process every session); README.md:37-46 shows a streamable-http config; microsoft-learn-mcp.md:13-43 shows a third (claude mcp add CLI) plus a .mcp.json example that does not exist in the plugin (the server is inline in plugin.json). A reader cannot tell which is canonical. The mcp-remote bridge also adds avoidable startup overhead vs native HTTP transport.

issues.title: plugin.json missing optional metadata; license mismatch
issues.detail: plugin.json has name/version/description/author but no homepage, repository, license, or keywords. README declares MIT license (README.md:103-105) but plugin.json carries no license field, and there is no LICENSE file in the plugin directory.

issues.title: README architecture claims contradict dual-database positioning
issues.detail: README 'Architecture Preferences' states 'PostgreSQL - Primary database' while the plugin description, SKILL.md provider-selection section, and v1.1.0 release all position PostgreSQL and SQL Server as equal first-class options.

improvements.title: Restore trigger-rich frontmatter description with EN + TH triggers
improvements.detail: In SKILL.md:3, re-add the pre-8948bc1 'Use when:' clause and English trigger list (.NET, C#, Entity Framework, EF Core, migration, repository pattern, unit of work, Clean Architecture, Aspire, Web API, DbContext, LINQ, Blazor, SQL Server, PostgreSQL) and add Thai triggers consistent with sibling plugins (e.g. "สร้างโปรเจกต์ .NET", "เขียน C#", "ทำ migration", "ออกแบบ entity", "สร้าง Web API"). This is the single highest-leverage fix for auto-triggering and brings frontmatter back in line with README.md:81-90.

improvements.title: Fix MCP tool documentation to name the real tools and delete the mcporter fallback
improvements.detail: In SKILL.md:51-68 replace the mcporter bash snippet and 'mcp__microsoft-learn__search' with the actual tools: microsoft_docs_search (overview), microsoft_code_sample_search (code samples), microsoft_docs_fetch (full page), and a one-line workflow (search -> code sample -> fetch). In references/microsoft-learn-mcp.md remove the 'Alternative: mcporter CLI' section (lines 207-241) and correct line 229. Keeps the model from shelling out to a failing npx command.

improvements.title: Rewrite README install section and add version + changelog
improvements.detail: Replace README.md:16-31 with the correct commands (/plugin marketplace add <this repo>, /plugin install dotnet-dev@agent-marketplace) and fix the standalone-skill copy path (plugins/dotnet-dev/skills/dotnet-dev). Add a version header (v1.2.0) and a short changelog covering 1.0.0 (initial), 1.1.0 (SQL Server + multi-database), 1.2.0 (MCP/metadata fixes), matching the convention used by ui-mockup/long-running READMEs.

improvements.title: Slim SKILL.md via a references/code-templates.md split and dedupe alternatives
improvements.detail: Move inline patterns 4-8 (generic Repository implementation, DbContext, DependencyInjection, appsettings examples, Aspire AppHost - SKILL.md:171-400) into a new references/code-templates.md, keeping only BaseEntity/IRepository/IUnitOfWork signatures and principles in SKILL.md (~50% size cut). While doing so: state explicitly that AuditableEntityInterceptor (ef-core-patterns.md) and the SaveChangesAsync override are mutually exclusive choices; keep retry-on-failure config in one place only.

improvements.title: Add a two-sided integration section documenting long-running and system-design-doc handoffs
improvements.detail: dotnet-dev currently references zero other plugins (grep confirms no matches for long-running/system-design-doc/feature_list/design_doc_list anywhere under plugins/dotnet-dev). Add a 'Works with' section to SKILL.md and README: (1) long-running /init and /continue invoke this skill for .NET projects; (2) system-design-doc hands off Entities -> C# models, Relationships -> EF configurations, Data Dictionary -> migrations (per system-design-doc SKILL.md:451-461); optionally instruct the skill to check for .design-docs/design_doc_list.json and derive entity classes from registered design docs when present, making the contract executable rather than conceptual.

improvements.title: Fix template defects in BaseEntity and Repository soft-delete
improvements.detail: In SKILL.md: remove BaseEntity<TKey>'s 'public new TKey Id' shadowing (make Id strategy explicit via a single generic base or document the long-only default), and drop the manual !e.IsDeleted predicates from Repository methods since the global query filter already handles soft delete (or remove the filter and keep predicates - one mechanism only, with a note about IgnoreQueryFilters() for admin queries).

improvements.title: Switch bundled MCP server to native HTTP transport and consolidate config docs
improvements.detail: Change plugin.json mcpServers to {"type": "http", "url": "https://learn.microsoft.com/api/mcp"} to eliminate the npx/mcp-remote node process spawned every session, then make README.md:33-46 and microsoft-learn-mcp.md:11-43 show that single canonical config (delete the .mcp.json example that doesn't exist in the plugin). Also note the potential duplicate with an externally installed microsoft-docs plugin exposing identical tools.

improvements.title: Complete plugin.json metadata
improvements.detail: Add homepage/repository (the AgentMarketPlace repo), license: "MIT" (to match README.md:103-105), and keywords mirroring the marketplace tags, so the manifest is self-describing when installed outside this marketplace.

analysis.summary: brain v3.0.0 is a skills-only plugin (14 skills, no commands/, no agents) wrapping the external graph-brain MCP server with a "Brain First" strategy: SessionStart preload hook, 4-step search protocol, note versioning via changelog notes, deep 10-phase codebase scanner, and cross-session activity logging. Versions are consistent between plugin.json and marketplace.json (3.0.0, fixed in commit 4891e31), and the SessionStart hook is a model of safety (static cat, exit 0, explicit never-block rules). However, the plugin has no README (the only marketplace plugin besides bigbrain without one), does not bundle or document the graph-brain MCP server it is 100% dependent on, references its core GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md by bare filename that skills cannot resolve at runtime, and its update/versioning protocol uses save-knowledge instead of the server's update-knowledge/get-note-history/restore-note-version tools, risking note duplication. Integration is one-directional but real: ui-mockup, long-running, and flow-discovery all query/save via graph-brain MCP tools, though flow-discovery's saves violate brain's own Graph Protocol save rules.
strengths: Exemplary SessionStart hook: hooks/hooks.json uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, session-start.sh is a static cat + exit 0 (never blocks, near-zero cost), and the injected context explicitly mandates 'NEVER block the session' with graceful degradation to codebase reading when the MCP server is down
Version consistency: plugin.json 3.0.0 == marketplace.json 3.0.0 (recently synced in commit 4891e31), and brain-help's 'Graph Protocol (v3.0.0)' label matches

Token-efficient skills: 13 of 14 SKILL.md files are 1.8-7.8KB (largest, brain-scan, is 14.5KB); content in English with Thai-output mandate, consistent structure (frontmatter, Steps, Thai report templates) across all skills

GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md as a single source of truth for save rules (projectName, >=2 tags, folderPath convention, duplicate check, wiki links), 4-step search escalation (text -> tags -> graph traversal -> similar), and the versioning/changelog protocol — a genuinely good design intent
Connection-failure handling is specified in every entry path (hook, /brain, /brain-load, /brain-scan Phase 1, /brain-status) with retry/skip options — never blocks the user
brain-scan Smart Scan is thoughtful: git-diff-based incremental detection, changed-file-to-phase mapping table, deduplication, deleted/renamed-file handling, and user confirmation before large scans
Cross-session observability via .brain/activity-log.json (gitignored) with a dedicated /brain-log viewer and per-command structured log entries

Genuinely consumed by the ecosystem: ui-mockup /create-html-mockup, long-running /init + /continue, flow-discovery /flow-research, and system-design-doc /help all reference brain queries or graph-brain MCP tools

issues.title: Plugin is 100% dependent on the graph-brain MCP server but neither bundles nor documents it
issues.detail: Every skill and the SessionStart hook call mcp__graph-brain__* tools, yet plugin.json has no mcpServers entry, there is no .mcp.json in the plugin, and there is no README with setup instructions. A fresh marketplace install yields 14 skills and a hook that all fail until the user manually configures the MCP server — and nothing in the plugin tells them how. Repo-wide grep shows 'graph-brain' in JSON only in .claude/settings.local.json (user-level config) and marketplace.json tags.
issues.evidence: D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (no mcpServers key); no .mcp.json under D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/; D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/hooks/session-start.sh:7 instructs calling mcp__graph-brain__brain-stats with no install path documented anywhere in the plugin

issues.title: No README.md — only marketplace plugin (besides bigbrain) without one
issues.detail: All sibling plugins (ui-mockup, long-running, qa-ui-test, system-design-doc, flow-discovery, dotnet-dev) ship README.md. brain has none, so the marketplace entry's claims (incremental scan, permission matrix, cross-project intelligence) have no user-facing documentation, there is no changelog to cross-check the 3.0.0 version against, and no prerequisites section for the MCP server. Root README.md contains zero brain mentions. Git history confirms a README never existed (including in the brain-dev era).
issues.evidence: Directory listing of D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/ shows only GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md, hooks/, skills/, .claude-plugin/; grep -i brain D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/README.md returns nothing

issues.title: GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md referenced by bare filename — skills cannot resolve it at runtime
issues.detail: Six skills say 'Follow rules in `GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md`' with no path. The file lives at the plugin root, not inside any skill directory, so a relative read resolves against the user's project cwd and fails; ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} is never used in any SKILL.md. Since the protocol carries the load-bearing contracts (save rules, versioning steps, search escalation), skills silently degrade to their partial inline copies when the file can't be found.
issues.evidence: D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/skills/brain-scan/SKILL.md:12, skills/brain-save/SKILL.md:12, skills/brain-update/SKILL.md:12, skills/brain-search/SKILL.md:12, skills/brain-explain/SKILL.md:12, skills/brain-history/SKILL.md:86 — all bare 'GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md' references; file actually at D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md

issues.title: Versioning Protocol updates notes via save-knowledge; server's update-knowledge / get-note-history / restore-note-version are never used
issues.detail: GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md Step 3 ('Update Original Note') and brain-update/brain-save both instruct calling mcp__graph-brain__save-knowledge with new content to 'update' an existing note. The graph-brain server exposes a dedicated update-knowledge tool (plus get-note-history and restore-note-version), implying save-knowledge creates notes — so every 'update' likely produces a duplicate note instead of mutating the original. brain-history also hand-rolls history via changelog-note searches and manual restore instead of get-note-history/restore-note-version. Verify against server semantics; if save-knowledge upserts by title, document that explicitly.
issues.evidence: D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/brain/GRAPH_PROTOCOL.md:59 ('เรียก mcp__graph-brain__save-knowledge ด้วย content ใหม่'); skills/brain-update/SKILL.md:39; skills/brain-save/SKILL.md:58-64; skills/brain-history/SKILL.md:27-31 and 81-87; mcp__graph-brain__update-knowledge / get-note-history / restore-note-version appear in the server tool list but in zero skill files (grep confirms)

issues.title: Skill descriptions lack trigger phrases — weak auto-invocation for a 'Brain First' plugin
issues.detail: All 14 descriptions are short English one-liners with no 'USE THIS SKILL when...' trigger list and no Thai triggers, unlike the house style in qa-ui-test, flow-discovery, long-running, and dotnet-dev. For a plugin whose whole premise (and the user's recorded preference) is that Claude should query brain before exploring the codebase, the main brain skill's description gives the model few hooks to self-trigger on phrases like 'โปรเจกต์นี้ทำอะไร', 'หา dependency', 'permission ของ role'.

issues.title: Non-standard frontmatter fields user_invocable and args in all 14 skills
issues.detail: Only brain and bigbrain use 'user_invocable: true' and 'args:' — neither is a documented SKILL.md frontmatter field, so they are likely ignored (argument hints never surface to users). issues.title: brain-scan phase ordering is confusing: 'Phase 10.5' runs before 'Phase 10'
issues.detail: The versioning pass is numbered 10.5 yet documented and executed BEFORE the Phase 10 report ('Phase 10.5: Versioning (NEW — runs before Report)'). A numbered sequence where 10.5 precedes 10 invites the model to run them in numeric order and version notes after reporting counts, making the reported changelogs_created wrong.

issues.title: Hook matcher misses clear/compact, and bash dependency is a Windows portability risk
issues.detail: hooks.json matcher 'startup|resume' means after /clear (or compaction) the brain preload context is gone and never re-injected. The hook also shells out to bash, which on the developer's own win32 platform requires Git Bash on PATH — if absent the hook fails silently and Brain First never activates.

analysis.version: 2.1.0 (plugin.json + marketplace.json agree; README footer claims 1.3.0; help.md claims v1.7.0)
analysis.summary: Mature, contract-heavy documentation plugin (21 commands, 1 skill, no hooks) at v2.1.0 with a well-designed machine-readable registry (design_doc_list.json schema 2.3.0, split per-section layout, AC-NNN/UC-NNN ID contracts, sitemap.json graph schema). Core generation/sync/validation commands and templates are internally consistent, and the two heaviest consumers (long-running v2.9.0, ui-mockup) correctly implement the split-aware reading protocol. The main problems are documentation drift — README is frozen at v1.3.0 and help.md at v1.7.0, both missing the 9 v2.x commands — plus a partially executed v2.1 migration (system-design-doc.md and reverse-engineer.md still describe legacy single-file output), three commands with no frontmatter at all, and a qa-ui-test contract that is one-sided (qa-trace never looks in .design-docs/, qa-tracker template uses the forbidden AC-1.1 format, and use_case_id does not exist on the qa side).
strengths: Machine-readable contract design: skills/system-design-doc/templates/design_doc_list.json (schema 2.3.0) embeds sections[] registry with per-section anchors regexes, AC/UC structures, ID-format metadata (ac_id_format, uc_header_pattern), and compatible_with version gates — consumers can resolve everything without parsing prose

Split-layout progressive disclosure for consumers is real and adopted: SKILL.md 'Reading Protocol for Consumers' is implemented by long-running (skills/long-running/SKILL.md:668,698; coding-agent-guide.md:79) and ui-mockup (references/reading-design-docs.md:8-9) — token-efficient section-level reads instead of whole-doc cats
No hooks directory — zero per-session overhead; all weight is opt-in via commands/skill

Sitemap commands correctly use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for intra-plugin schema/template paths and pair them with concrete ajv-cli validation (commands/sitemap-init.md:40,52; sitemap-validate.md:22; sitemap-add-node.md:65)

Section templates are contract-consistent: all 10 templates/sections/NN-*.md carry the exact line-1 marker '<!-- sdd-section: <key> | doc: __PROJECT_SLUG__ | schema: 2.3.0 -->' matching split-design-doc.md Step 3 and CRITICAL RULE 36

CRITICAL RULES with numbered, testable constraints (rules 24-30 pin AC-NNN format and the UC heading regex that qa-trace greps) plus self-check checklists give downstream agents enforceable contracts rather than vague guidance
plugin.json and marketplace.json versions are in sync at 2.1.0, and the marketplace tags are accurate

Bidirectional sync commands document exactly which fields they write on both sides (e.g., sync-with-mockups.md:176-188 lists design_doc_list.json and mockup_list.json field updates that match the actual ui-mockup template fields)

The Commands table (lines 34-45) lists only 10 commands, omitting /brainstorm-design, /sync-with-qa-tracker, /help, /split-design-doc and all 8 /sitemap-* commands. README shows design_doc_list schema_version 2.1.0 (line 365) and 'Compatible Versions: design_doc_list.json 2.1.0' (line 398) vs actual template schema 2.3.0. Output Files section (lines 213-218) shows only the legacy single-file layout, contradicting the v2.1 default split layout. Cross-Plugin Integration section (lines 340-358) shows only 3 plugins, omitting qa-ui-test which /validate-integration covers. Changelog stops at 1.3.0.

issues.title: Three commands have no YAML frontmatter at all

issues.title: qa-ui-test side of the AC/UC contract is skewed: wrong search path, forbidden ID format in template, missing use_case_id field

Additionally /sync-with-qa-tracker Step 5 writes scenario.use_case_id, a field that appears nowhere in qa-ui-test (template or commands). issues.title: Intra-plugin template/reference paths mostly lack ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}

analysis.version: 2.9.0 (plugin.json + marketplace.json); SKILL.md frontmatter says 2.6.0; README says 2.0.0; help.md says 2.6.0+/2.8.0
analysis.summary: long-running v2.9.0 is the most contract-rich plugin in the marketplace: 19 commands, a 7-step Verification Pipeline, 4 release gates wired to qa-ui-test's qa-tracker.json, design-doc impact checks wired to system-design-doc's design_doc_list.json, and a UI Control Manifest handoff whose test categories match qa-ui-test exactly. The core machinery (continue/nfr-check/qa-coverage-check/emit-control-spec/scan-changes) is solid and genuinely two-sided. The plugin's weakness is severe internal documentation drift: README is frozen at v2.0.0 (7 minor versions behind, missing 7 commands, recommending two skills that do not exist), SKILL.md frontmatter says 2.6.0 and the skill body has zero mention of v2.7 scan-changes or v2.8 Gate 4, six commands have no frontmatter, no intra-plugin path uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, and the headline v2.9.0 feature (split-layout design-doc resolution) was added only to SKILL.md and references/coding-agent-guide.md while continue.md — the command that actually executes Verification Pipeline Step 2 — still uses the legacy single-file find fallback and never points to the guide.
strengths: plugin.json and marketplace.json versions are in sync at 2.9.0 (D:/GitHub/AgentMarketPlace/plugins/long-running/.claude-plugin/plugin.json:3, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json:67)

Gate 4 control manifest contract is exactly mirrored on the qa-ui-test side: mandatory categories render-binding/api-binding/permission/validation/cascade-loading-error in continue.md:1014-1024 match qa-ui-test/skills/qa-ui-test/references/control-spec-scenarios.md:15-33 verbatim

qa-tracker.json schema requirement (>=1.7.0) in nfr-check.md:78 and qa-coverage-check.md:68 matches qa-ui-test's shipped template (qa-tracker.json template declares schema_version 1.7.0 with nfr_results and traceability sections)

Strict consumer discipline: nfr-check.md:39 and qa-coverage-check.md:41 declare read-only on qa-tracker.json with idempotent re-runs; ID propagation rules (AC-NNN from system-design-doc, BUG-NNN from qa-ui-test, long-running only mirrors) documented in SKILL.md:756-759

Enforcement design is unusually rigorous: self-check checklists, output rejection criteria, and override flags (--force-coverage/--force-nfr/--force-bug-verify/--force-control-coverage) that require audit logging (continue.md:19-71, 1026-1049)
Has a written cross-plugin regression charter (tests/INTEGRATION_TEST.md) that explicitly tracks contract drift between /add-feature, /edit-feature and system-design-doc's /sync-with-features
No hooks directory — zero per-session overhead; all weight is opt-in via commands/skill
scan-changes.md is a well-designed upstream traceability enforcer with proper argument parsing ($ARGUMENTS), ignore patterns, and legacy-commit cutoff

SKILL.md is 42,749 bytes (~11k tokens) loaded on every skill trigger with full Verification Pipeline, model-assignment, and gate detail inline instead of in references/ — violating progressive disclosure. The skill description also lacks concrete EN+TH trigger phrases ('USE THIS SKILL when... / Thai triggers:') that sibling plugins qa-ui-test and flow-discovery use.

alignment: Clean Architecture structure (Domain/Application/Infrastructure/WebApi + tests) ใน SKILL.md ตรงกับสไตล์ที่ผู้ใช้ถนัด และสอดคล้องกับ guidance ของ Microsoft (eShopOnContainers architecture docs) — แยก layer ชัด, IEntityTypeConfiguration + ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly ถูกต้อง
Repository + Unit of Work เป็น first-class pattern ตามที่ผู้ใช้ต้องการ — มี interface, generic implementation, DI registration ครบ (SKILL.md sections 2-6)

รองรับ PostgreSQL และ SQL Server คู่กันจริงตลอดทั้ง plugin: provider switch ใน DependencyInjection, appsettings ตัวอย่างทั้ง 2 แบบ, references/ef-core-patterns.md มี section เฉพาะของแต่ละ DB (JSONB/array/FTS ฝั่ง Postgres; Temporal Tables/RLS/HierarchyId/Always Encrypted ฝั่ง SQL Server) และ database-agnostic configuration pattern

EF Core query patterns ใน references/ef-core-patterns.md ส่วนใหญ่ยังตรงกับ official docs ปัจจุบัน: AsNoTracking สำหรับ read-only, AsSplitQuery สำหรับ collection includes, projection แทน SELECT *, หลีกเลี่ยง N+1, ExecuteUpdate/ExecuteDelete สำหรับ bulk ops, compiled queries, SaveChangesInterceptor สำหรับ audit — ยืนยันกับ EF Core 10 docs แล้วว่ายังเป็น best practice

Testing strategy ตรงกับคำแนะนำ official: EF docs (ef/core/testing/choosing-a-testing-strategy) แนะนำ test กับ database จริง — testing-patterns.md ใช้ xUnit + Testcontainers (PostgreSQL) + Respawn + WebApplicationFactory + test builders ซึ่งเป็นแนวทางที่ถูกต้อง
Aspire เป็นส่วนหนึ่งของ stack ตามที่ผู้ใช้ต้องการ — AppHost เลือก Postgres/SqlServer ได้, ServiceDefaults + OpenTelemetry + health checks ตรงโครงสร้างมาตรฐาน

Domain-first + Rich Domain Model + Value Objects + Domain Events + soft delete + audit trail ตรง enterprise style ของผู้ใช้, async/await + CancellationToken ทุก method, EnableRetryOnFailure ทั้ง 2 provider
เนื้อหาเขียนเป็นภาษาไทยผสม technical English ตรงกับ preference ผู้ใช้ และ bundle Microsoft Learn MCP มาให้ตรวจ docs ล่าสุดได้ (plugin.json mcpServers)

gaps.area: Aspire ทั้งไฟล์ references/aspire-setup.md
gaps.current: เป็น Aspire 8.x-era ทั้งหมด: dotnet new aspire-starter, Aspire.* 8.* packages, ไม่มี WaitFor/ContainerLifetime, และ `builder.AddDockerComposeEnvironment("infra", "./docker-compose.yml")` ใช้ signature ที่ไม่มีจริง (API จริงไม่รับ path compose file สำหรับ import)
gaps.recommended: ปัจจุบันคือ Aspire 13.x (Aspire.Hosting v13.1.0): ติดตั้งผ่าน Aspire CLI (`aspire new`, `aspire run`, `aspire update`), AppHost ใช้ `Sdk="Aspire.AppHost.Sdk/13.0.0"` ไม่ต้อง reference Aspire.Hosting.AppHost เอง, เพิ่ม `.WaitFor(database)`, `.WithLifetime(ContainerLifetime.Persistent)`, `.WithHttpHealthCheck("/health")`; upgrade path 8.x→9.x→13
gaps.source: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire/get-started/upgrade-to-aspire-13 และ API docs view=dotnet-aspire-13.0 (AddPostgres remark: "the dependent resource will wait until the Postgres resource is able to service requests" ผ่าน WaitFor)

gaps.area: Generic Repository — IQueryable leak ขัดกับเหตุผลที่มี repository
gaps.current: IRepository<T> มี `IQueryable<T> Query()` — EF testing docs ระบุชัดว่าถ้าทำ repository เพื่อ mock/stub ได้ ห้าม expose IQueryable ("your repository cannot expose any IQueryable-returning methods... IEnumerable should be returned instead") และ official stance คือ DbContext implement Repository+UoW อยู่แล้ว, "Repositories shouldn't be mandatory", repository ควรเป็น per-aggregate
gaps.recommended: ผู้ใช้ยืนยันจะใช้ Repo+UoW → เก็บ pattern ไว้ แต่ทำให้ถูก: (1) per-aggregate repository interface ใน Domain layer ต่อยอดจาก generic base, (2) query เฉพาะทางเป็น method ที่ return ผลลัพธ์จริง (IReadOnlyList/DTO) ไม่ leak IQueryable, (3) เพิ่มกล่อง trade-off ตรงๆ ใน SKILL.md: ถ้า leak IQueryable จะ mock ไม่ได้และ abstraction ไม่คุ้ม — ทางเลือกคือยอมรับว่า unit test ชั้น query ต้องเป็น integration test (Testcontainers)

gaps.area: UnitOfWork transactions ชนกับ EnableRetryOnFailure (bug จริง)
gaps.current: SKILL.md pattern #6 เปิด EnableRetryOnFailure ทั้ง 2 provider พร้อมกับ pattern #3 ที่ UoW มี BeginTransactionAsync/Commit/Rollback — เมื่อใช้ retrying execution strategy การเรียก BeginTransaction ตรงๆ จะ throw InvalidOperationException (execution strategy ไม่รองรับ user-initiated transactions)
gaps.recommended: เพิ่มตัวอย่าง `var strategy = _context.Database.CreateExecutionStrategy(); await strategy.ExecuteAsync(async () => { await using var tx = ...; ...; await tx.CommitAsync(); });` และ note ว่า SaveChangesAsync ครั้งเดียวเป็น atomic อยู่แล้ว ไม่ต้องเปิด transaction เพิ่มในเคสปกติ

gaps.area: API documentation — Swashbuckle ล้าสมัย
gaps.current: แนะนำ Swashbuckle.AspNetCore 6.* + AddSwaggerGen/UseSwagger/UseSwaggerUI (SKILL.md NuGet section, aspire-setup.md Program.cs)
gaps.recommended: .NET 9+ มี built-in OpenAPI: `builder.Services.AddOpenApi()` + `app.MapOpenApi()` (Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi) — Swashbuckle ถูกถอดจาก templates แล้ว; ถ้าต้องการ UI ใช้ Scalar/Swagger UI เสริม; `.WithOpenApi()` ถูก deprecate ใน .NET 10 (ASPDEPR002) ให้ใช้ AddOpenApiOperationTransformer
gaps.source: https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/openapi/overview ("In .NET 9 and later... replaces Swashbuckle as the default. Swashbuckle is no longer included in project templates")

gaps.current: SKILL.md DI ลงท้ายด้วย AddStackExchangeRedisCache อย่างเดียว ไม่พูดถึง HybridCache
gaps.recommended: แนะนำ HybridCache (Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Hybrid, GA): AddHybridCache() ใช้ Redis ที่มีอยู่เป็น L2 อัตโนมัติ ได้ stampede protection + tag invalidation + L1 in-memory ฟรี; มี Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Postgres เป็น L2 option ฝั่ง Postgres ด้วย — เข้ากับ dual-DB ของผู้ใช้พอดี

gaps.area: เวลา — IDateTimeService / DateTime.UtcNow กระจัดกระจาย
gaps.current: ef-core-patterns.md ใช้ custom IDateTimeService; SKILL.md ใช้ DateTime.UtcNow ตรงๆ ใน BaseEntity initializer, Repository.Update, SaveChangesAsync override (ตั้ง audit ซ้ำซ้อนกับ interceptor ใน reference อีกไฟล์)
gaps.recommended: ใช้ TimeProvider (.NET 8+, built-in) inject ผ่าน DI + FakeTimeProvider (Microsoft.Extensions.TimeProvider.Testing) ใน tests; ASP.NET Core เลิกใช้ ISystemClock ไปแล้ว; เลือก audit ที่เดียว (interceptor — เป็นแนวทาง official) ลบ override ซ้ำใน DbContext

gaps.area: Minimal APIs vs Controllers — ไม่มี guidance เลย
gaps.current: SKILL.md เอ่ยถึง "Minimal APIs" ใน stack list แต่ทุก template เป็น controller-based ไม่มีเกณฑ์เลือก
gaps.recommended: Official: "Start with Minimal APIs for new projects"; controllers เหมาะเมื่อ需 model binding extensibility, advanced validation, application parts, OData — ผู้ใช้ถนัด MVC controllers ดังนั้น flag trade-off ตรงๆ: ใช้ controllers ต่อได้ (รองรับเต็มที่ใน .NET 10) แต่ service/microservice ใหม่เล็กๆ ควรพิจารณา Minimal APIs + route groups; เพิ่ม decision table ใน SKILL.md

gaps.area: Soft delete — ยังไม่ใช้ EF Core 10 named query filters + filter ซ้ำซ้อน
gaps.current: DbContext สร้าง global query filter ด้วย expression tree เอง และ Repository ทุก method ยัง filter !e.IsDeleted ซ้ำอีกชั้น; IgnoreQueryFilters จะปิด filter ทั้งหมด (รวม tenant filter) ทีเดียว
gaps.recommended: EF 10 มี named query filters: `HasQueryFilter("SoftDeletionFilter", b => !b.IsDeleted)` + `IgnoreQueryFilters(["SoftDeletionFilter"])` เลือกปิดเฉพาะตัวได้ — เหมาะกับ soft delete + multitenancy พร้อมกัน; ตัด filter ซ้ำใน repository ออก

gaps.area: EF Core 10 features ใหม่ที่ขาด
gaps.current: ef-core-patterns.md หยุดที่ EF 7-8 era: OwnsOne สำหรับ value objects, ExecuteUpdate แบบ expression-tree, JSONB เฉพาะ Postgres
gaps.recommended: เพิ่ม: (1) ComplexProperty + .ToJson() สำหรับ value objects (แนวทางใหม่แทน OwnsOne), (2) ExecuteUpdateAsync รับ regular lambda — ทำ conditional SetProperty ได้แล้ว, (3) SQL Server 2025/Azure SQL `json` data type (UseAzureSql / compat level 170) เทียบเท่า JSONB — ปิด gap dual-DB, (4) parameterized collection translation ใหม่, (5) LeftJoin/RightJoin LINQ operators

gaps.area: Concurrency สำหรับ PostgreSQL
gaps.current: ef-core-patterns.md section 4 มีแต่ [ConcurrencyCheck]/[Timestamp] RowVersion ("SQL Server preferred") — ไม่มีทางเลือกฝั่ง Postgres ทั้งที่ผู้ใช้รองรับ 2 DB
gaps.recommended: เพิ่ม Npgsql `xmin` system column: `builder.Property(p => p.Version).IsRowVersion()` / UseXminAsConcurrencyToken สำหรับ optimistic concurrency บน PostgreSQL
gaps.source: Npgsql EF Core provider docs (concurrency tokens) — npgsql.org/efcore + https://learn.microsoft.com/ef/core/saving/concurrency

gaps.current: testing-patterns.md: xunit 2.*, Testcontainers 3.*, Mvc.Testing 8.*, FluentAssertions 6.*, มีแต่ Testcontainers.PostgreSql (postgres:16-alpine) — ไม่มี SQL Server fixture ทั้งที่ skill ชู dual-DB; ctor ของ CustomWebApplicationFactory เรียก StartAsync().GetAwaiter().GetResult() (sync-over-async)
gaps.recommended: อัปเดตเป็น xunit.v3, Testcontainers 4.x + เพิ่ม Testcontainers.MsSql fixture + Respawn SqlAdapter.SqlServer, Mvc.Testing 10.*, เพิ่ม FakeTimeProvider ใน test patterns, ใช้ IAsyncLifetime แทน sync-over-async; เพิ่ม Aspire.Hosting.Testing (DistributedApplicationTestingBuilder มีอยู่แล้วแต่ version เก่า)

gaps.area: Licensing ของ libraries ที่แนะนำ (trade-off ต้องบอกตรงๆ)
gaps.current: แนะนำ MediatR 12.*, AutoMapper 13.*, FluentAssertions 6.* โดยไม่เตือนว่า MediatR (v13+), AutoMapper (v15+) และ FluentAssertions (v8+) เปลี่ยนเป็น commercial license ตั้งแต่ปี 2025
gaps.recommended: เพิ่ม note: version ที่ pin ไว้ยังเป็น OSS ใช้ฟรีได้ แต่จะไม่ได้ feature/security updates ใหม่; ทางเลือก: ซื้อ license, ใช้ Mapperly (source-gen, ฟรี) แทน AutoMapper, manual mapping ใน projection (เร็วกว่าและ AOT-friendly), Shouldly/AwesomeAssertions แทน FluentAssertions, หรือ custom mediator/Wolverine แทน MediatR — ให้ผู้ใช้ตัดสินใจเอง
gaps.source: Ecosystem fact (ไม่ใช่ Microsoft docs) — ตรวจจาก GitHub announcements ของแต่ละ project; ควร verify version ล่าสุดก่อน commit

gaps.area: MCP integration — tool names ผิดและ transport อ้อม
gaps.current: SKILL.md/references/microsoft-learn-mcp.md อ้าง tool `mcp__microsoft-learn__search` และสอนใช้ mcporter CLI ผ่าน npx; plugin.json ใช้ `npx mcp-remote` (ต้องพึ่ง Node)

gaps.current: SKILL.md ~480 บรรทัด รวม principles + code templates เต็มๆ 8 ชุด + migration commands + package lists ไว้ body เดียว — โหลดทั้งหมดทุกครั้งที่ skill trigger; frontmatter description ไม่มี "Use when/triggers" (อยู่แค่ใน marketplace.json)

gaps.area: Code bugs เล็กใน templates
gaps.current: (1) `BaseEntity<TKey>` ใช้ `public new TKey Id` ซ่อน property ฐาน — EF map พังง่ายและเป็น anti-pattern, (2) `await _dbSet.AddAsync(...)` ทั้งที่ docs แนะนำ Add ปกติ (AddAsync จำเป็นเฉพาะ HiLo value generators), (3) README ยังอ้าง path `dotnet-dev-skill` / `your-username` placeholder
gaps.recommended: (1) กลับด้าน: `BaseEntity<TKey>` เป็นตัวหลัก แล้ว `BaseEntity : BaseEntity<long>`, (2) ใช้ `_dbSet.Add(entity)` พร้อม comment เหตุผล, (3) อัปเดต README ให้ตรง repo จริง (AgentMarketPlace marketplace)
gaps.source: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.entityframeworkcore.dbcontext.addasync (remarks: ใช้เฉพาะกรณี value generator ต้อง async)

recommendations.detail: แก้ SKILL.md บรรทัด 24 เป็น ".NET 10 (LTS ปัจจุบัน, support ถึง Nov 2028)"; เปลี่ยน package pins ทั้งหมด 8.* → 10.* (Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer, Caching.StackExchangeRedis, Mvc.Testing); เพิ่ม note สั้นๆ เรื่อง LTS/STS cadence เพื่อให้ skill ไม่ล้าสมัยเร็ว ("ใช้ LTS ล่าสุด — ตรวจกับ microsoft_docs_search ก่อนสร้าง project")

recommendations.title: เขียน references/aspire-setup.md ใหม่เป็น Aspire 13
recommendations.detail: แทนที่เนื้อหา 8.x ทั้งไฟล์: ติดตั้ง Aspire CLI + `aspire new`/`aspire run`/`aspire update`, AppHost csproj แบบ `Sdk="Aspire.AppHost.Sdk/13.0.0"` (net10.0), เพิ่ม `.WaitFor(database)` + `.WithLifetime(ContainerLifetime.Persistent)` + `.WithHttpHealthCheck()` ใน AppHost ตัวอย่าง dual-DB, ลบ `AddDockerComposeEnvironment("infra", "./docker-compose.yml")` ที่ signature ผิด, และเพิ่ม pattern สำคัญที่ขาด: `builder.EnrichNpgsqlDbContext<ApplicationDbContext>()` / `EnrichSqlServerDbContext` เพื่อใช้ Aspire ร่วมกับ AddInfrastructure() ที่ register DbContext เองอยู่แล้ว (ตอนนี้ aspire-setup.md กับ SKILL.md DI ขัดกัน — จะ double-register DbContext)

recommendations.title: เพิ่ม section "Repository/UoW ให้ถูกวิธี + trade-offs ตรงไปตรงมา" ใน SKILL.md
recommendations.detail: เก็บ Repo+UoW ไว้ตามสไตล์ผู้ใช้ แต่เพิ่ม: (1) per-aggregate repository (ICustomerRepository : IRepository<Customer>) interface อยู่ Domain/Application layer, (2) เปลี่ยน Query() ที่ return IQueryable → method เฉพาะทางที่ return IReadOnlyList/paged result พร้อมกล่องเตือน: "EF docs: repository ที่ expose IQueryable จะ mock ไม่ได้ — ถ้าจะคง Query() ไว้เพื่อความสะดวก ให้ยอมรับว่า test ชั้นนั้นต้องเป็น Testcontainers integration test", (3) แก้ UoW transaction ให้ wrap ด้วย CreateExecutionStrategy().ExecuteAsync เพราะตอนนี้ชนกับ EnableRetryOnFailure (โค้ดปัจจุบัน throw runtime), (4) ย่อหน้า honesty note: Microsoft ระบุว่า DbContext คือ Repository+UoW ในตัว และ repository "ไม่ใช่ข้อบังคับ" — ใช้เพราะทีมถนัดและช่วย mock ได้ ไม่ใช่เพราะเป็น requirement

recommendations.title: แทนที่ Swashbuckle ด้วย built-in OpenAPI
recommendations.detail: SKILL.md NuGet section: ตัด Swashbuckle.AspNetCore 6.* → Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.*; ตัวอย่าง Program.cs ใช้ AddOpenApi()/MapOpenApi() + ตัวเลือก Scalar.AspNetCore สำหรับ UI; เพิ่ม note ว่า WithOpenApi() deprecated (.NET 10, ASPDEPR002) ใช้ AddOpenApiOperationTransformer แทน; อัปเดต aspire-setup.md Program.cs ตัวอย่างด้วย

recommendations.title: แก้ MCP tool names + เปลี่ยน transport ใน plugin.json
recommendations.detail: plugin.json: เปลี่ยน mcpServers.microsoft-learn จาก npx mcp-remote → {"type": "http", "url": "https://learn.microsoft.com/api/mcp"} (ตัด Node dependency); SKILL.md + references/microsoft-learn-mcp.md: แก้ชื่อ tool เป็น microsoft_docs_search / microsoft_docs_fetch / microsoft_code_sample_search, ตัด mcporter/bash workaround sections ทิ้ง, เพิ่มตัวอย่างใช้ code_sample_search หา snippet จริง

recommendations.title: เพิ่ม EF Core 10 patterns ใน references/ef-core-patterns.md
recommendations.detail: เพิ่ม sections: named query filters สำหรับ soft delete + tenant filter พร้อม IgnoreQueryFilters(["..."]) แบบเลือกปิด (แทน expression-tree generator ใน SKILL.md และตัด !IsDeleted ซ้ำใน repository), ComplexProperty + ToJson() แทน OwnsOne สำหรับ Money value object, ExecuteUpdateAsync แบบ regular lambda (conditional updates), SQL Server 2025 json type + UseAzureSql เทียบ JSONB (ปิด gap dual-DB), Npgsql xmin concurrency token คู่กับ RowVersion ของ SQL Server

recommendations.title: เพิ่ม references/dependency-injection.md (cross-cutting สมัยใหม่)
recommendations.detail: ครอบคลุม: TimeProvider แทน IDateTimeService/DateTime.UtcNow (แก้ BaseEntity initializer + interceptor ให้รับ TimeProvider, เลือก interceptor เป็น audit ทางเดียว ลบ SaveChangesAsync override ซ้ำ), keyed services (.NET 8+) — เหมาะกับ dual-DB scenario เช่น [FromKeyedServices("reporting")], options pattern เต็มรูป (AddOptions<T>().BindConfiguration().ValidateDataAnnotations().ValidateOnStart()), HybridCache แทน IDistributedCache ตรงๆ (Redis เป็น L2), IExceptionHandler + ProblemDetails (.NET 8+) แทน custom middleware

recommendations.title: เพิ่ม references/react-integration.md + auth-security.md
recommendations.detail: ผู้ใช้ทำ React frontend กับ ASP.NET Core เสมอแต่ plugin ไม่มีเรื่องนี้เลย: react-integration.md (CORS policy, Vite dev proxy, Aspire AddViteApp/AddNpmApp orchestration, openapi-typescript จาก built-in OpenAPI document, cookie vs JWT สำหรับ SPA, BFF pattern); auth-security.md (JWT bearer config, ASP.NET Core Identity + API endpoints, .NET 10 behavior change: API endpoints return 401/403 ไม่ redirect login, authorization policies)

recommendations.title: อัปเดต references/testing-patterns.md
recommendations.detail: xunit 2.* → xunit.v3, Testcontainers 3.* → 4.*, Mvc.Testing 8.* → 10.*, เพิ่ม MsSqlContainer fixture + Respawn SqlAdapter.SqlServer สำหรับฝั่ง SQL Server (ตอนนี้ test ได้แค่ Postgres ทั้งที่ขาย dual-DB), แก้ CustomWebApplicationFactory sync-over-async เป็น IAsyncLifetime, เพิ่ม FakeTimeProvider examples, เพิ่ม licensing note: FluentAssertions 8+/MediatR 13+/AutoMapper 15+ เป็น commercial — version ที่ pin ยังฟรีแต่ freeze; เสนอ Shouldly/Mapperly/manual mapping เป็นทางเลือกพร้อม trade-offs ให้ผู้ใช้เลือกเอง

recommendations.title: เพิ่ม decision guidance: Minimal APIs vs Controllers
recommendations.detail: เพิ่ม decision table ใน SKILL.md: official แนะนำ Minimal APIs สำหรับ project ใหม่ (เร็วกว่า, boilerplate น้อย) แต่ controllers ยังเหมาะกับ enterprise app ที่ผู้ใช้ถนัด (model binding extensibility, filters, OData, ทีมคุ้น MVC) — ระบุชัดว่านี่คือจุดที่ preference ผู้ใช้ต่างจาก default guidance และทั้งคู่ supported เต็มที่; แนะนำ route groups + endpoint filters เมื่อเลือก Minimal APIs

recommendations.title: เพิ่ม references/deployment.md
recommendations.detail: Aspire 13 deployment: `aspire publish` / azd init + azd up สำหรับ Azure Container Apps, docker-compose publisher, Kubernetes manifests, EF migration bundles (dotnet ef migrations bundle) + dedicated migration service pattern สำหรับ production (สอดคล้องกับ efcore-patterns skill ที่ผู้ใช้มีอยู่แล้วในเครื่อง)
